reviews.title: Cute and functional

Positives

Negatives

reviews.title: I'm not keen on the cover material
reviews.text:
While the Arae Electronic Organizer does what it says, its implementation isn't great.

The double layers of this product has one and a half flap of elastic bands to hold bundled up wires, that's 18 spots total. The rest are two large pockets and a small pocket. That's plenty of room to carry most charging accessories that you'd have with you if you were equipped with tablets and phones. For laptops and cameras, you'll need other solutions. Even action camera chargers may have a hard time fitting in this slim product.

It's not a problem that this thing is pretty much made out of nylon from the outside in but the material that makes up the exterior of this product makes it look cheap. It's a rough, synthetic material that just makes it a bit cheap looking. I think cloth or leather would have been a better choice.

In addition, the zipper sticks, making it hard to quickly open this up to retrieve your wires for charging.

reviews.title: Case closed
reviews.text:
It's pretty amazing how much this will hold and still close up properly. I am using it for many cables and some adapters, and I am also keeping those little instruction manuals that come with everything in one of the zipper pouches. You can keep a lot of cables and adapters in this, and it will still zip up and be small enough for travel.
I like this case a lot.

reviews.title: Smaller than expected
reviews.text: Water proof? Not going to try that. This is super slim so you can't really fit a whole lot in. Wires, maybe... but devices, like portable routers, not exactly. Slim portable chargers, sure. Higher capacity batteries, nope. Won't fit anything more than two iPhones thick.

reviews.title: No More Ziplock Bags
reviews.text: This is such a better alternative to tossing cables into a ziplock bag.  The nice bag with zipper allows me to keep everything in one place.  I wished the compartments were a little wider, but it's easy to just use two compartments. Nice quality bag, once I get spares I would just pack these with a spare ready and be ready to go anytime.  Nice organization addition to my travel gear.

reviews.title: Handy little case
reviews.text: Does a good job at cutting down on cable clutter. I took a black Sharpie and identified the various cords by writing abbreviations on the cloth stretch band that secures the cords. It also helps organize miscellaneous adapters and charging units. Not sure how long it will last but for organizing cords and small electronic items it’s a good buy.

reviews.title: Good sound and battery life, touch activated button bad
reviews.text: Overall, this is a good earbud. However, if you hold it in your hand you run the risk of your skin/touch activating the voice commands, it hears a random snippet of what you’re saying and it calls someone from your address book that kinda sorta sounds like what you had said and you have to quickly try to hang up as your phone dials someone you haven’t talked to in 5 years, you whispering “oh f*** nononono” over and over until it hangs up and you pray it didn’t actually connect on their side but it does every damn time and then you’re forced into a conversation of pleasantries. So like...yeah it works well, good range but the touch activated button was a big no from me.

reviews.title: Not was I was expecting
reviews.text: The reviews raved about this, but I got a different pair. There was no clip, the packaging boasted 10 hours of bluetooth. The mic didn't work for phone calls (too fuzzy). I returned these. I think their getting rid of a later edition of these headphones that aren't as good as the ones they've previously sold. You can see other reviews saying the same thing. There's no noise cancelation either. So yeah, I think they're just lying about the ones they send you.

reviews.title: These are great!

reviews.title: ... used it I got complaints of how I sounded like I was in a

reviews.title: These Don't last.
reviews.text:
I was pretty sad to discover that these literally lasted one road trip from Oregon to Southern California. Roughly 20hrs of drive time so I used them for music and audio books. The sound quality was okay but not great. If we had a lot of road noise I couldn't hear anything on full volume
When I tried to use them for the return trip they were even quieter and the microphone wouldn't do more then a whisper on the other end. We tried multiple phones and multiple carriers to see if that was the issue
Another issue on the 2nd round was the amount of feedback we had on the line when calling or even listening to music. I thought my phone was broken or something till I turned my Bluetooth off and just used my phone speaker. I was really excited for this but won't buy them again.

reviews.title: I do not recommend these earbuds.  They have a serious design flaw.
reviews.text:
I would have easily given these earbuds 4 or 5 stars, if it wasn't for the "low battery" warning. JLAB clearly put no thought into this. Here are my issues:

1) The first low battery warning occurs about 5 minutes before the battery dies . So if you just started your jog, or just got on the bus, you're screwed. With 6 hours of play time, the low battery notification should give you at least 20 minutes notice. Since you are almost always "out and about" when listening to headphones, it would be nice to have enough juice to finish your activity or get where you are going.

2) The low battery message (which is verbal) repeats FREQUENTLY for the brief remainder of your battery life, constantly interrupting your music. This is so annoying you wind up taking the earbuds off.

Since the earbuds are essentially useless once you get the first low battery warning, the warning serves no purpose. Giving the low battery warnings at the 30, 20 and 10 minute marks would be far less annoying and actually give you a chance to get to a charger.

reviews.title: Cheap Buys Cheap
reviews.text: Not sure what I expected for so little, so shame on me. I'll tell you these made my 9-year-old's ears hurt. I'm not sure how they could cancel any noise, as there is no buffer. I tried the ones she put on and they are very uncomfortable. Returning.

reviews.title: Great product and great price.
reviews.text: We bought a pack of these for my son because he goes through headphones crazy fast; since we bought these, he's still on the first pair. We will be buying more in the future if we have to.

reviews.title: They are priced low enough that a defective or marginal one doesn't sour the price.
reviews.text: Quality control could be a little better, but the price for the multipack is tough to beat.

reviews.title: For the cost… I am super happy with them.
reviews.text: I’m giving these earbuds five stars because they exceed my expectations when they only cost seventy cents each. I find it hard to complain about these, especially considering the cost. The sound is quite adequate for such earbuds. I use these in a classroom setting for kids who either cannot afford earbuds or forget there is at home. They are individually packaged. Nice assortment of colors. Though they are being sold by a US company, I do not show where they are made. I can only assume they are made in China.

reviews.title: Great Sound Just Wish they had a Microphone
reviews.text: Fun colors and love this type of ear plug over the cone shaped style. Listening to music on my phone sounds great  but I wish they had a microphone so I could easily answer calls. Now I have to unplug the earphones when the phone starts to ring and then re-plug after I'm done on the call. I guess they are really designed for use on exclusive music players. The listing is confusing because it says "Bluetooth" which they don't have, but the wire is about 4ft. long so your music unit can easily be kept anywhere convenient. No volume control either so that has to be handled on your phone/music player.

reviews.title: Amazing ear pad replacements for the Logitech G35 headset!
reviews.text:
- Makes my G35 headset look better than when I bought it new thanks to the red accent markings!
- The installation tool was extremely helpful, and thanks for including it.
- Just a bit of warning for others the kit that includes the head pad isn’t mean for the Logitech G35 headset, because it is a different size and it doesn’t have Velcro underneath the head pad. I just ordered the ear pads only, and ended up using the spare two fake leather head pads that came with my G35 headset.
- Other thoughts: Not sure what some people are complaining about, but my ear pads are comfortable and fit my headset well. I’m just going to assume some peoples ears are extremely sensitive than others to certain fabrics. I like the cloth mesh pads compared to the original leather pads for two reasons. The cloth won’t crack or peel off like the leather pads, and the cloth pads are more breathable for the ears (ears won’t get hot or humid).

reviews.title: They are comfortable, which is the main thing
reviews.text: I bought these for my G930 since the outside was flaking off of mine a lot. I have been using these for almost 5 months and I have had no issues out of them. They are comfortable, which is the main thing. And since it isn't the fake leather of the original, I dont have a problem with it causing my ears to sweat or stick to it. Installing them were simple as well.

reviews.title: Barely noticeable difference between these pads and the official Logitech pads
reviews.text: The only difference in these pads is that the mesh is slightly rougher than that of the ear pads that come with the Logitech G430 headphones. Otherwise, there is no noticeable difference.

reviews.title: great replacement for my G930
reviews.text: I bought these blue mesh ear pads to replace my original fake leather pads on my Logitech G930 headphones. These went on super easily, took no effort, and are a perfect fit. They also come with a little tool to help slide the pads on but i didn't even need it. I also like the mesh pads because my ears stay a bit cooler than on the original leather-ish ones. These are a great value for the price, and I would definitely recommend them.

reviews.title: Good but scratchy.
reviews.text: They fit perfectly on my Logitech g35s but they are very scratchy.

title: FiiO Q5S High Resolution DAC and Headphone Amplifier
features: FiiO Q5S high resolution DAC and headphone amplifier

reviews.title: The little DAC with big magic
reviews.text:
This gadget is simply ahead of its time. For audio enthusiasts (like me) who dare to explore how the right DAC can elevate the experience of music listening to a delightful level, this DAC won't disappoint. It brings out the best in headphones (reasonably decent ones), which in my case were a Sennheiser HD 600 and a budget triple-driver IEM. Also, it breathed life into the premium speakers of my old car--simply unbelievable; I never heard them sound so good (the DAC's line-out was fed into the car's aux-in). My tracks came from Amazon HD Music.

As a gadget aficionado in general, I find this DAC well-designed, functionality- and aesthetics-wise, and with a solid build and classy finishing that clearly distinguish it from the other DACs in this price range. It's one of the best gadgets I bought during recent years.

reviews.title: No quality control.
reviews.text: Ordered a “like new” used item. Description said it had a 4.4mm headphone jack.  The box was a relatively new, but item inside was nothing like described.

reviews.title: Much better than expected...Updated!!! MQA use...
reviews.text:
02/16/21
I've been using the Q5s TC for some weeks now mainly with Apple Music and sometimes the SIRIUSXM app for iOS on my iPhone 12 Pro. Just a days ago I decided to check out MQA music on TIDAL. I have multiple headphones and settled on Shure SRH1540 and SRH1840. I have listed a number of headphones in other reviews so I won't go there. Anyway, WOW!!!, what a revelation. Listening to MQA's through TIDAL is very revealing and at one point on some recordings I had to say to myself, "there were anymore detail and layering I would be able to taste and smell it". I can also easily hear what I can assume is in some cases budgets, engineering and anything else that might affect the way a recording sounds.

Now from my understanding the Q5s does not unpack the MQA music file but it appears. it's definitely seeing and recognizing the file, if not totally able to unpack it. I say this because the indicator on the front glows a solid yellow which according to FiiO is AptX HD, don't know but it makes a difference. Talking about spacial clues? all over the place. But of course it depends on the recording. I can say this, listening to all my old new music, from Aretha to Soundgarden and more is so much fun and enjoyable.

I'm a ex-audiophile that actually love music more than the equipment (most of the time). The Apple AirPods Max reviews and rages made me revisit headphone collection. After selling 3 and decommissioning 1 I ended up with my mostly hated (not by me, I love the way sound) Sennheiser HD700. Measurements and other people's bias' aside I decided to put a dac/amp in my setup for my iPhone 12 Pro. The Q5s plugs right into my iPhone via the supplied connector with no issues at all. The bluetooth works great as well. I prefer direct connection over using BT mainly because I want to as close to the true signal as possible. AAC sounds great through this dac/amp combo. I can't hear a real difference the 2. The Q5s is a solid and professional quality dac/amp combo. It does get a little warm while running but nothing that concerns me. I'm also using the Shanling UP4 which is to my ears good too and serves another purpose in modest collection. The Shanling is not capable of plugging directly into the iPhone but does a really decent job with BT. I prefer the direct connection ability of the Q5s. Of course like I mention the build quality is in a different league as it should be due to the price difference between the 2.

reviews.title: Ain’t loud enough...
reviews.text: It sounded low volume, muffled & cluttered to me. Didn’t sound like an *amp* with dual chips. BT wasn’t strong enough either.

reviews.title: They fixed all of my complaints with the Q5
reviews.text:
1) One USB port
2) Volume knob is also the power-on, as found on the Q1
3) Nice input selector on the right with some labeled LEDs that also show battery charging level
4) Fewer buttons
5) Bluetooth 5.0 can do 24-bit/96khz, so need cable only for charging once Apple supports aptX HD (audiophile-only issue)

Before going any further, I upgraded the firmware from 0.04 to the latest (0.39). The frequency response with 0.39 seems a lot smoother.

The Q5s sounds different from Q5. It's a clearer, more detailed sound. I also like that the bass level is a little higher with the OFF setting. I use ATH-M50x headphones.

So, FiiO, if you are listening, here are my new suggestions:

a) Replace the microUSB port with USB-C. It's a much more reliable connection. microUSB gets loose over time.
b) Have a bass switch or knob that allows for more bass settings. Actually, the EQ feature on the FiiO app is probably a better way to go, assuming it is remembered when not using the app. I have not tried it yet. If that's the case, why not get rid of the base boost button altogether?
c) Have different sleep timeouts for charging vs. not charging. Add 1 hour, 2 hour, and Never as possibilities to each.

Non-FiiO problem: macOS and iOS do not support Bluetooth aptX HD or LDAC, so I will need to wait for 24-bit/96khz over BT 5.0. I submitted a bug to Apple. I hope others will do the same.

reviews.title: Just What I Wanted
reviews.text: I wanted to replace my old clunker of a boom box.  I needed a small CD player with_ a speaker so I wouldn't have to wear ear buds.  That's what I found here. At first, I wondered about the sound, but considering how small it is, the sound is fine.  I'm glad to have easier access to my CD collection.

reviews.text:
I have a few CDs that I have kept despite mostly using streaming services. Music not available online. This device allows me to hear them again.
Connected very easy to my Bluetooth tailgate speaker.

Don't plan on using the built in speaker as I have several better speakers that I can connect to with cable or Bluetooth.

reviews.text:
I found using the Aux cable along with my Bluetooth headphones the sound quality is much better.
The headphones that came with unit wasn't that good.

reviews.title: Very Good Portable CD Player With Plenty of Options
reviews.text:
With the onset of Pandora, satellite radio and many other such options, I found I wasn’t playing my CDs anymore. In fact, I couldn’t find my CD player! And the high-end system in my truck doesn’t even have a CD option. So when I saw this I figured I give it a try. I like that it’s portable and plays without skipping even when bumped, running with it, or the dog knocking it off of the table. It can be used with my several different sets of earphones. It also plugs into my truck stereo without a problem.

I also wanted it to play through its own speaker while in my crafts room. This is where the 5th star gets removed. When turning it up nearly all the way the speaker starts to buzz on certain notes. It isn’t loud at all on its own either so using it without auxiliary speakers is not good if more than about 8 feet from it. However, I am playing my CDs which I have not heard in years, and that makes me very happy.

reviews.title: Fun and good
reviews.text:
My 13 yo son is on the vintage wave, so he has been using this and the cassette one. He is happy!
Very portable, small size.
The sound is ok.
Has been using it non stop and still working good.
Would be a fun gift for someone who likes old school (on a fun way, not like a collector)
We like it.

reviews.title: Difficult to connect. Oddly LOW VOLUME and INTERFERES with apps like VIBER and DUO. Easy to loose.
reviews.text:
Where do I start:::
Connecting, if you want to use it with multiple devices, you can just forget it (if connected to one device, they will not allow you to connect to another device until you actually delete the connection from the current device even if it's off, did not have this issue with Apple Pods).
SOUND LEVEL is oddly LOW. Seriously, even at max setting does not even come close to even cheap plug-in buds, so can't use them in any loud place. Noise canceling does not help with that.
If used with call apps like Viber or Duo you'll be missing every other word. Don't know how or why, but connection signal goes to crap while these are on. As soon as I switch to plug-in buds, everything works just fine.
TOO DAMN SMALL. Sure it's convenient for the ear, but trying to handle them like putting in or taking out, better be sure to do it somewhere safe in case you drop them (not outside for sure). It would be better if they had over the year clips or something.
COLLECTS all your EAR-WAX. Yes, these things go in so deep that it just gets absolutely gross (never had this problem with other buds and I clean my ears every day)

Call, track change, and volume controls are all within close reach through one main button. 22 hours of audio on a single charge means you spend less time recharging and more time enjoying your music. Jib True earbuds are adventure-ready, too. An IPX4 rating means Jib True is sweat- and dust-resistant. You don’t need to worry about damage if a little rain kicks up during your urban or outdoor adventure. Dual microphones make your calls more clear and if you feel like keeping one ear open, you can use either bud ‘solo’ without missing a beat.
features: Supreme Sound - Powered by 6mm drivers the Jib features dynamic audio with gnarly bass and a wide sound range. The earbuds also feature bluetooth 5.0 for a strong signal making sure you can listen to your music or take phone calls interruption free.

22 Hours of Play - With a 55mAh battery in the earbud and 500 mAh in the charging case you'll get a whopping 22 hours of charge total, 6 in the earbuds and 16 in the case. Use the included charging case for on the go charging as well as safe storage for your earbuds.

Dual Microphones - keeping you sounding extra clear while on calls or talking to your best friend Siri. Dual mics also means you can have 1 ear open and 1 earbud in while on calls to keep aware of your surroundings.

Small, Medium, Large Ears - Included in the Jib is 3 different eargel tips to make sure you have comfy noise isolating fit. The earbuds are super lightweight and water/dust resistant perfect for the active person!
Buy With Confidence - 1 year manufacturer's warranty included with the Jib, be sure to wear your earbuds with full confidence.

reviews.title: Tis Loud! Has great bass & long lasting battery!
reviews.text:
Alot of headphones lack good bass and sometimes cuts out, but not this set, these earbuds have great bass without over doing it.

Not a bad price to boot, and they're Skullcandy, nough said

reviews.title: Was expecting more from these
reviews.text: I love Skull Candy. But honestly I was expecting more and a better sound. They actually hurt my ears so they were a no go for me. Gave them to a niece.

reviews.title: I love Skullcandy Products!
reviews.text: Must BUY NOW!!💪👍

reviews.title: Good sound
reviews.text: They sound great. But they don't stay in the ear very well. I would buy this brand again. Maybe not this  Style

reviews.title: Amazing for the price... Great range, sound good.
reviews.text:
Battery latest me my whole work day.

Sound is good enough, bluetooth range is impressive, better then my Samsung buds.

Easy setup.

So far, very happy. Some people say I sound like im in an empy room, but some times it doesnt happen?

I dont know if its true noise canceling, but the buds are tight enough you dont get outside noise.

title: Konnek Stein Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth Ear Buds in-Ear Headband Headphones, 6 Hrs Playtime V5.0 Wireless Headset Sport Earbuds w/Mic for Gym Running Compatible with iPhone Samsung Android, Black
description: Bluetooth Version 5.0 Wireless Range 8m Battery Type 110mAh with protect circuit Charge Voltage 5V DC microUSB Music Play Time Up to 6 hours Charging Time 2-3 hours speaker Φ13，32Ω，3mW SPL 103±3dB 0.1CM/1KHz micphone Omni-directional ，-39±3dB Total length of line 61.8cm±2cm weight 14.5g
features: Advanced Bluetooth V5.0 Connectivity: Wireless bluetooth earbuds, performs faster transmission speeds and more stable connections with iOS iphone and Android smart phones, tablets, laptops, MP3 players.

High-Quality Music: This ear buds wireless bluetooth earbuds featuring upgraded acoustic components, it delivers balanced bass and clear treble for a rich, high-fidelity sound experience. You can have a better music experience with earphones.

Sports Design Concept: Featherlight and fit perfectly in your ears without putting pressure on your neck. It can improve your wearing comfort when exercising. Perfect for gym, workout and running etc. You don't have to worry about losing these bluetooth headphones.

6-Hour Playtime: Earbuds wireless bluetooth keep your music hassle-free by wirelessly linking your enabled device and streaming from up to 30 feet away. With up to 6 hours playtime of mind-blowing sound that’ll leave you with good vibes all day long.

Built-in Microphone: This earbud & in-ear headphones is built in with high quality Microphone, and it is much convenient for you to answer calling for hands-free, no need to hold the phone in hand. You would feel its brilliants for your work and daily tasks.

reviews.title: Exact type of ear buds I need.
reviews.text: These ear buds are hard to find anymore.  I love the ability to pull one bud out and leave it dangle vs the newer ear buds that you have to pocket if you take out.  I wish they had gel pieces that enter your ears as I have small ears so they get a little sore after several hours of wearing.  But I will take it as I can get the ease of use I want from them.  Volume, sound and use of buttons are all fantastic.
